headerTitle: Senior Full Stack Engineer  ·  LLM-Enabled Agentic Products  ·  Third-Party Integrations  ·  Construction AI
subjectText: Senior Full Stack Engineer — Trunk Tools, Inc. (Remote US) · $120K–$190K
intro: Trunk Tools is scaling AI agents across construction jobsites — intelligent document Q&A expanding into core operational workflows. The role is hands-on feature work on the LLM-enabled agentic product and building the integration layer that connects Trunk to the systems contractors already use. Both are live in my stack today.
bullets: LLM-enabled agentic product engineering — 14 live AI endpoints using multi-model routing (Claude Haiku/Sonnet/Opus + GPT-4o), Zod-validated structured output, MCP protocol, HITL confirm-before-execute, and agentic workflow orchestration. Production reliability, not demos.

Next.js 15 + TypeScript + Node.js + PostgreSQL — exact stack, live across 13 platforms. 100+ API routes, BullMQ + Redis job queues, Prisma ORM, Docker/PM2/Nginx. Real users, real operational conditions.

Third-party integration connectors — Stripe, Google OAuth2, Twilio IVR, Sinch SMS, IMAP servers, TCAD appraisal APIs, webhook pipelines. Auth edge cases, rate limiting, retry logic, and bidirectional sync across eight industry verticals.
matchNotes: Direct stack match (Next.js 15, TypeScript, Node.js, PostgreSQL). LLM agentic product engineering is live in my stack. Construction domain is new — but document Q&A and operational workflow AI is the same architecture pattern. NYC/Austin/Remote = workable.
